items.title: NEC werkt met Chery toe naar kraker tegen 'supersterk' Ajax
items.summary: NEC maakt zich op voor een bezoek aan Ajax. De Nijmegenaren staan op de derde plek, met evenveel punten als de ploeg van Fred Grim. Het duel tussen de nummer drie en vier van de Eredivisie belooft een spannende ontmoeting te worden. Trainer Dick Schreuder blikt vooruit.

items.title: Bosz onder de indruk van mentaliteit Pepi: 'Ik was bij hem in het ziekenhuis'
items.summary: Ricardo Pepi kan zaterdag terugkeren in de wedstrijdselectie van PSV. Dat zou dan relatief snel zijn na een gebroken arm. Trainer Peter Bosz is ervan overtuigd dat de mentaliteit van de spits daar een rol in speelt. 'Hij was alleen maar bezig om zo snel mogelijk weer op het veld te staan.'

items.title: North London derby buildup, Europa League reaction, Premier League team news and more – live
items.summary: ⚽ Latest news, previews and updates before the weekend ⚽ 10 things to look out for | And email Tom Eddie Howe had some words of praise for Pep Guardiola and Manchester City: ““Genuinely, I think we learn something more about ourselves and our game going forward every time we play them. They have been the benchmark for a number of years for many teams. Each painful defeat we suffer at the Etihad we try to grow from it, evolve and improve. “They have been very, very good and are led by an outstanding manager. They continue to be the benchmark in my opinion.” Continue reading...

items.title: Russell admits concern over Hamilton's fast Ferrari practice starts
items.summary: George Russell has admitted his concern over Mercedes' inferiority at race starts compared to Ferrari's Lewis Hamilton ahead of the opening race of the 2026 Formula 1 season.

items.title: Madrid send UEFA evidence in Vini Jr. racism probe
items.summary: Real Madrid say they have supplied "all available evidence" to UEFA as the governing body investigates allegations of racism made by Vinícius Júnior.

items.title: Fabregas 'not proud' of incident with Milan player
items.summary: Como coach Cesc Fabregas has apologised for making contact with an AC Milan player near the touchline in Wednesday's 1-1 league draw at the San Siro.

items.title: 19 sentenced to jail for AFCON final hooliganism
items.summary: A Moroccan court Thursday sentenced 19 soccer fans to up to a year in prison on charges of hooliganism stemming from a chaotic Africa Cup of Nations final between Morocco and Senegal that saw supporters try to storm the field.

items.title: Man United ease past Atlético to book Bayern QF
items.summary: Manchester United set up a Women's Champions League quarterfinal against Bayern Munich with a thumping 5-0 aggregate win over Atlético Madrid.

items.title: Top 50 USMNT players of 2026, ranked by club form: Which Americans made the top 10?
items.summary: Who are the 50 best American players of 2026 so far? The top 10 sees a shake-up as the USMNT Player Performance Index returns.

items.title: Rooney ziet perfecte opvolger van Guardiola bij Manchester City
items.summary: Wayne Rooney heeft alvast vooruitgekeken naar het tijdperk ná Pep Guardiola bij Manchester City. Het Britse voetbalicoon heeft de perfecte vervanger voor de Catalaanse succesmanager gevonden: Vincent Kompany.

items.title: Piastri confirms manager Webber to attend fewer F1 races
Waarom Bosz zich totaal geen zorgen maakt over een nieuwe winterdip van PSV
items.summary: Peter Bosz is totaal niet bang dat PSV net zo'n terugval krijgt als afgelopen seizoen. De trainer van de landskampioen ziet een duidelijk verschil tussen het PSV van nu en het PSV van toen.

items.title: Rentree aanstaande: Gavi sluit aan bij groepstraining van Barcelona
items.summary: Positief nieuws uit de ziekenboeg van Barcelona: Gavi (21) heeft vrijdag voor het eerst weer meegedaan aan de groepstraining van de Catalanen. Op sociale media delen de Blaugrana verheugd beelden van zijn eerste sessie in maanden. De groeibriljant van Barça was lang uit de roulatie met een knieblessure.

items.title: Selfies and juggling day jobs: Chatham Town land ‘amazing’ Women’s FA Cup tie
items.summary: Fourth-tier team, the lowest-ranked left in the competition, and their Cup-winning manager are hoping to stun Birmingham Old-school sweet dispensers stand tall pitchside at the Bauvill Stadium, almost as if watching the action. Behind them, Bobby’s bar is bustling and above the club logo on a wall behind one of the goals are four unmissable words: “If I can dream …” Helping to manifest that dream is the shiny Women’s FA Cup trophy, temporarily on display in the boardroom. The group of players who have just finished a day’s work are about to begin their training session at 8.30pm. Chatham Town Women, the lowest-ranked side in the fifth round of the Women’s FA Cup and one of only two sides remaining from below the second tier, are preparing for the game of their lives, against big-spending Birmingham City at St Andrew’s on Saturday, live on television, vying for a place in the quarter-finals. Chatham cover their players’ expenses but do not pay football salaries, unlike their full-time opponents. Continue reading...

items.title: Murillo hails Pereira after ‘problems’ with past Nottingham Forest managers
items.summary: There is ‘light at end of tunnel’ with new man in charge Defender hints at conflicts with Postecoglou and Dyche The Nottingham Forest defender Murillo has said the arrival of Vítor Pereira represents “the light at the end of the tunnel” and admitted the squad had problems with previous managers. Pereira got off to a dream start at Forest with a 3-0 Europa League playoff first-leg victory at Fenerbahce on Thursday, in which Murillo opened the scoring. The Brazilian praised Pereira for creating “magic” despite having only three training sessions. Murillo said the Portuguese head coach, who has a contract until 2027, had transformed the spirit within an underperforming squad. The 57-year-old, who rescued Wolves last season, is Forest’s fourth head coach of the season. Continue reading...

items.title: 'Emotional' Mourinho should apologise - McCarthy
items.summary: Benni McCarthy says Jose Mourinho should admit his "mistake" following the Benfica manager's post-match comments after Real Madrid's Vinicius Jr claimed he was racially abused.

items.title: Weekend Winners: Backing Boiling Point to cook up a storm in Winter Derby
items.summary: It is Winter Derby weekend on Sky Sports Racing as Lingfield takes centre stage this Saturday and the Weekend Winners team all landed on different horses in the feature.

items.title: Rangers' Rohl has 'total belief' in tight race
items.summary: Danny Rohl has "total" belief Rangers can win the Scottish Premiership but has called on his players to back it up with "consistency".
